title: Codes for "Protocol to quantify high-dimensional energy landscape of oscillatory systems using diffusion decomposition"
author: Shirui, Bian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Here are two codes corresponding to the protocol entitled "Protocol to quantify high-dimensional energy landscape of oscillatory systems using diffusion decomposition".  The protocol describes the specific steps for quantifying the energy landscape of two 2-dimensional stochastic oscillatory systems with additive and multiplicative noise, respectively. However, we can also apply this protocol to general high-dimensional oscillatory systems (see "Bian et al., Cell Rep. Phys. Sci., Volume 6 Issue 2, 102405" for the technique of the diffusion decomposition of Gaussian approximation (DDGA)  ).  
  Here, we provide two specific 2-dimensional examples for display, including a general Stuart-Landau system with additive noise (related to Land_DDGA_Protocol_SL.m), and a chemical Langevin system with multiplicative noise (related to Land_DDGA_Protocol_CLE.m). Users can directly run all these codes on MATLAB 2023a or other suitable version.  
  For general use, users only need to enter the stochastic dynamics (and the corresponding Jacobian matrix as a function) in the first block of the codes, and the procedure will automatically generate the corresponding probability distribution (and the energy landscape obtained by logarithmic mapping) through the DDGA approach.

The protocol describes the specific steps for quantifying the energy landscape of two 2-dimensional stochastic oscillatory systems with additive and multiplicative noise, respectively. However, we can also apply this protocol to general high-dimensional oscillatory systems (see "Bian et al., Cell Rep. Phys. Sci., Volume 6 Issue 2, 102405" for the technique of the diffusion decomposition of Gaussian approximation (DDGA)  ).  
  Here, we provide two specific 2-dimensional examples for display, including a general Stuart-Landau system with additive noise (related to Land_DDGA_Protocol_SL.m), and a chemical Langevin system with multiplicative noise (related to Land_DDGA_Protocol_CLE.m). Users can directly run all these codes on MATLAB 2023a or other suitable version.  
  For general use, users only need to enter the stochastic dynamics (and the corresponding Jacobian matrix as a function) in the first block of the codes, and the procedure will automatically generate the corresponding probability distribution (and the energy landscape obtained by logarithmic mapping) through the DDGA approach.
